description: Deduplicated public concept, method, tool, value, and publication rows from InstituteOS tech trees.

records.label: Action as Active Inference
records.summary: Action as the process of changing the world to fulfill predictions, minimizing free energy by acting on the environment rather than updating beliefs.

records.label: Learning as Model Update
records.summary: Learning as the slow update of generative model parameters to minimize long-term free energy, encompassing synaptic plasticity and structure learning.

records.label: Perception as Inference
records.summary: Perception understood as Bayesian inference — updating internal generative models to explain away prediction errors from sensory input.

records.label: Active Inference
records.summary: The process theory derived from the Free Energy Principle: agents act to fulfill predictions and minimize expected free energy through perception and action.

records.label: Allostasis
records.summary: Predictive homeostasis — anticipatory regulation that adjusts setpoints based on expected future demands rather than reacting to deviations.

records.label: Autopoiesis
records.summary: Self-producing systems as defined by Maturana and Varela — living systems that continuously produce and maintain themselves.

records.label: Entropy
records.summary: A measure of uncertainty or disorder in a probability distribution, connecting information theory to thermodynamics.

records.label: Expected Free Energy
records.summary: The objective function for policy selection in active inference, combining epistemic (information-seeking) and pragmatic (goal-seeking) drives.

records.label: Free Energy Principle
records.summary: Karl Friston's unifying theory proposing that all adaptive systems minimize variational free energy to maintain their structural and functional integrity.

records.label: Free Energy Principle (Core)
records.summary: The core statement: any system that persists must minimize variational free energy, an upper bound on surprisal, through perception and action.

records.label: Generative Models
records.summary: Probabilistic models specifying how observed data is generated from latent causes, central to both inference and learning under the FEP.

records.label: Homeostasis
records.summary: The maintenance of biological stability through regulatory feedback — keeping internal variables within viable bounds.

records.label: Markov Blankets
records.summary: Statistical boundaries that separate internal states from external states, defining the interface through which a system interacts with its environment.

records.label: Perception-Action Loop
records.summary: The bidirectional sensorimotor cycle through which agents continuously update beliefs (perception) and act on the world (action).

records.label: Precision Weighting
records.summary: Attention as the optimization of precision (inverse variance) on prediction errors, modulating the influence of sensory vs. prior information.

records.label: Predictive Processing
records.summary: The brain as a prediction machine — Helmholtz, Clark, and Friston's framework where perception is hypothesis testing against sensory input.

records.label: Self-Organization
records.summary: The emergence of order and structure without external direction, explained under FEP as free energy minimization at the system level.

records.label: Surprise Minimization
records.summary: The principle that adaptive systems minimize surprisal (negative log-evidence) to maintain their existence within expected states.

records.label: Bayesian Inference
records.summary: Inference via Bayes' theorem — updating prior beliefs with likelihoods from observed data to obtain posterior distributions.

records.label: Policy Selection
records.summary: Action selection via minimization of expected free energy over candidate policies, balancing exploration and exploitation.

records.label: Variational Bayes
records.summary: Approximate Bayesian inference via optimization of the evidence lower bound (ELBO), converting intractable integration into tractable optimization.

records.label: Karl Friston
records.summary: Originator of the Free Energy Principle and Active Inference framework; Professor at University College London.

records.label: Consciousness Theories
records.summary: Higher-order and global cognitive access theories of consciousness, explored through the lens of free energy minimization and self-modeling.

records.label: Information Theory
records.summary: Shannon entropy and mutual information — the mathematical foundations for quantifying uncertainty and information content.

records.label: Statistical Mechanics
records.summary: The thermodynamics of many-particle systems, providing the formalism for free energy, partition functions, and ensemble behavior.

records.label: RxInfer.jl
records.summary: Julia-based reactive message passing library for scalable Bayesian inference and active inference agent design.

records.label: pymdp
records.summary: Python library for simulating active inference agents using partially observable Markov decision processes (POMDPs).

records.label: Accessibility
records.summary: Institute value: making Active Inference Institute research and education accessible to diverse global communities.

records.label: Real-World Applicability
records.summary: Institute value: ensuring research translates into real-world applications that benefit individuals and communities.

records.label: Scientific Rigor
records.summary: Institute value: maintaining high standards of scientific rigor in all research and educational outputs.
